remarks.text: The NAT Gateway is created with Standard SKU. If no Public IP Address is explicitly associated via

summary.text: Associates an Azure PaaS resource with a Network Security Perimeter.

children.text: mode, resources within the perimeter can communicate with each other, but public access is restricted to the rules defined in the perimeter profile.
mode, traffic that would be blocked by the perimeter rules is logged but not denied. This is useful when onboarding resources to identify required access rules before switching to enforced mode.

returns.text: A reference to the target resource builder for chaining.

accessMode.text: The access mode for the association. Defaults to
to log violations without blocking traffic.

associationName.text: An optional name for the association. If not provided, defaults to

children.text: This method automatically creates the Private DNS Zone, VNet Link, and DNS Zone Group required for private endpoint DNS resolution. Private DNS Zones are shared across multiple private endpoints that use the same zone name.
When a private endpoint is added, the target resource (or its parent) is automatically configured to deny public network access. To override this behavior, use

summary.text: Provides extension methods for adding Azure Virtual Network resources to the application model.

members.name: AddAzureVirtualNetwork
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureVirtualNetworkResource> AzureVirtualNetworkExtensions.AddAzureVirtualNetwork(this IDistributedApplicationBuilder builder, string name, string? addressPrefix = null)

summary.text: Adds an Azure Virtual Network resource to the application model.

addressPrefix.text: The address prefix for the virtual network (e.g., "10.0.0.0/16"). If null, defaults to "10.0.0.0/16".

builder.text: The builder for the distributed application.

name.text: The name of the Azure Virtual Network resource.

examples.code:
var vnet = builder.AddAzureVirtualNetwork("vnet");
            var subnet = vnet.AddSubnet("pe-subnet", "10.0.1.0/24");

description.text: This example creates a virtual network with a subnet for private endpoints:

members.name: AllowInbound
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> AzureVirtualNetworkExtensions.AllowInbound(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> builder, string? port = null, string? from = null, string? to = null, SecurityRuleProtocol? protocol = null, int? priority = null, string? name = null)

summary.text: Adds an inbound allow rule to the subnet's Network Security Group.

remarks.text: If no Network Security Group has been associated with the subnet, one is automatically created.

from.text: The source address prefix (e.g., "AzureLoadBalancer", "Internet", "10.0.0.0/8"). Defaults to "*" (any).

name.text: The rule name. If not specified, auto-generated from parameters.

port.text: The destination port range (e.g., "443", "80-443"). Defaults to "*" (any).

priority.text: The rule priority (100-4096). If not specified, auto-increments from 100 by 100.

to.text: The destination address prefix. Defaults to "*" (any).

description.text: This example allows HTTPS traffic from the Azure Load Balancer:

members.name: AllowOutbound
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> AzureVirtualNetworkExtensions.AllowOutbound(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> builder, string? port = null, string? from = null, string? to = null, SecurityRuleProtocol? protocol = null, int? priority = null, string? name = null)

summary.text: Adds an outbound allow rule to the subnet's Network Security Group.

remarks.text: If no Network Security Group has been associated with the subnet, one is automatically created.

from.text: The source address prefix. Defaults to "*" (any).

name.text: The rule name. If not specified, auto-generated from parameters.

port.text: The destination port range (e.g., "443", "80-443"). Defaults to "*" (any).

priority.text: The rule priority (100-4096). If not specified, auto-increments from 100 by 100.

to.text: The destination address prefix (e.g., "Internet", "VirtualNetwork"). Defaults to "*" (any).

members.name: DenyInbound
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> AzureVirtualNetworkExtensions.DenyInbound(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> builder, string? port = null, string? from = null, string? to = null, SecurityRuleProtocol? protocol = null, int? priority = null, string? name = null)

summary.text: Adds an inbound deny rule to the subnet's Network Security Group.

remarks.text: If no Network Security Group has been associated with the subnet, one is automatically created.

from.text: The source address prefix (e.g., "Internet", "VirtualNetwork", "10.0.0.0/8"). Defaults to "*" (any).

name.text: The rule name. If not specified, auto-generated from parameters.

port.text: The destination port range (e.g., "443", "80-443"). Defaults to "*" (any).

priority.text: The rule priority (100-4096). If not specified, auto-increments from 100 by 100.

to.text: The destination address prefix. Defaults to "*" (any).

members.name: DenyOutbound
members.signature: public static IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> AzureVirtualNetworkExtensions.DenyOutbound(this IResourceBuilder<AzureSubnetResource> builder, string? port = null, string? from = null, string? to = null, SecurityRuleProtocol? protocol = null, int? priority = null, string? name = null)

summary.text: Adds an outbound deny rule to the subnet's Network Security Group.

remarks.text: If no Network Security Group has been associated with the subnet, one is automatically created.

from.text: The source address prefix. Defaults to "*" (any).

name.text: The rule name. If not specified, auto-generated from parameters.

port.text: The destination port range (e.g., "443", "80-443"). Defaults to "*" (any).

priority.text: The rule priority (100-4096). If not specified, auto-increments from 100 by 100.

to.text: The destination address prefix (e.g., "Internet", "VirtualNetwork"). Defaults to "*" (any).

remarks.text: This method automatically configures the subnet with the appropriate service delegation for the target resource type (e.g., "Microsoft.App/environments" for Azure Container Apps).

Represents an Azure Network Security Perimeter resource.

children.text: A Network Security Perimeter groups PaaS resources (such as Storage, Key Vault, Cosmos DB, and SQL) into a logical security boundary. Resources within the perimeter can communicate with each other, while public access is controlled by the perimeter's access mode and rules.

children.text: A Network Security Perimeter groups PaaS resources (such as Storage, Key Vault, Cosmos DB, and SQL) into a logical security boundary. Resources within the perimeter can communicate with each other, while public access is controlled by the perimeter's access mode and rules.

Represents an access rule configuration for an Azure Network Security Perimeter.

remarks.text: Access rules control how traffic flows into and out of the network security perimeter. Inbound rules specify which external sources (IP ranges or subscriptions) can access resources within the perimeter. Outbound rules specify which external destinations (FQDNs) resources within the perimeter can communicate with.

Represents a security rule configuration for an Azure Network Security Group.

remarks.text: Security rules control inbound and outbound network traffic for subnets associated with the Network Security Group. Rules are evaluated in priority order, with lower numbers having higher priority.

summary.text: Gets or sets whether network traffic is allowed or denied.
Gets or sets an optional description for the security rule.
Gets or sets the destination address prefix. Defaults to "*" (any).
Gets or sets a
whose value is resolved at deploy time and used as the destination address prefix for the rule. Takes precedence over
Gets or sets the destination port range. Use "*" for any, or a range like "80-443".
Gets or sets the direction of the rule.
Gets or sets the name of the security rule. This name must be unique within the Network Security Group.
Gets or sets the priority of the rule. Valid values are between 100 and 4096. Lower numbers have higher priority.
Gets or sets the network protocol this rule applies to.
Gets or sets the source address prefix. Defaults to "*" (any).
Gets or sets a
whose value is resolved at deploy time and used as the source address prefix for the rule. Takes precedence over
Gets or sets the source port range. Defaults to "*" (any).
Provides well-known Azure service tags that can be used as source or destination address prefixes in network security group rules.

children.text: Service tags represent a group of IP address prefixes from a given Azure service. Microsoft manages the address prefixes encompassed by each tag and automatically updates them as addresses change.
These tags can be used with the

description.text: Use service tags when configuring network security rules:

summary.text: Represents Azure App Service and Azure Functions service addresses.
Represents Microsoft Entra ID (formerly Azure Active Directory) service addresses.
Represents Azure Container Registry service addresses.
Represents Azure Cosmos DB service addresses.
Represents Azure Key Vault service addresses.
Represents the Azure infrastructure load balancer. This tag is commonly used to allow health probe traffic from Azure.
Represents Azure Monitor service addresses, including Log Analytics, Application Insights, and Azure Monitor metrics.
Represents Azure Traffic Manager probe IP addresses.
Represents Azure Event Hubs service addresses.
Represents the Gateway Manager service, used for VPN Gateway and Application Gateway management traffic.
Represents the Internet address space, including all publicly routable IP addresses.
Represents Azure Service Bus service addresses.
Represents Azure SQL Database, Azure Database for MySQL, Azure Database for PostgreSQL, Azure Database for MariaDB, and Azure Synapse Analytics.
Represents the Azure Storage service. This tag does not include specific Storage accounts; it covers all Azure Storage IP addresses.
Represents the address space for the virtual network, including all connected address spaces, all connected on-premises address spaces, and peered virtual networks.
